text: is the VOA Special English Technology Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音慢速英语科技报道节目。

text: We recently told you about a website called pastpages .org.
zh: [1] 我们最近向大家介绍过一个名为pastpages.org的网站。

text: It saves the home pages of 70 news websites from around the world every hour.
zh: [2] 该网站每小时保存来自世界各地70家新闻网站的主页。

text: That report led us to look for a website that saves images of newspapers from around the world.
zh: [3] 那篇报道促使我们去寻找一个能保存世界各地报纸版面图片的网站。

text: We found Today's Front Pages, a site operated by the Newseum.
zh: [4] 我们找到了《今日头版》，这是由新闻博物馆运营的一个网站。

text: The Newseum is a museum of news reporting and the media in Washington, D .C.
zh: [5] 新闻博物馆是位于华盛顿特区的一座新闻报道与媒体博物馆。

text: Paul Sparrow is Senior Vice President with the organization.
zh: [6] 保罗·斯帕罗是该机构的高级副总裁。

text: He says, one of the most interesting things about newspapers
zh: [7] 他说，关于报纸最有意思的一点是，

text: is that they are a snapshot in time.
zh: [8] 它们是某一时刻的快照。

text: They capture a moment when the people working with a newspaper
zh: [9] 它们记录下这样一个瞬间：报社的工作人员

text: say, these are the most important stories affecting our community.
zh: [10] 认定，这些就是影响我们社区的最重要新闻。

text: Paul Sparrow says visitors to the the Today's Front Pages website,
zh: [11] 保罗·斯帕罗说，访问《今日头版》网站的用户，

text: can choose to see all of its newspapers.
zh: [12] 可以选择查看所有报纸的版面。

text: Or they can look only at newspapers from one area of the world,
zh: [13] 也可以只查看世界某一地区的报纸，

text: such as Asia, Africa, or South America.
zh: [14] 比如亚洲、非洲或南美洲。

text: Visitors can also sort newspapers by alphabetic order, either by state or by country.
zh: [15] 用户还可以按字母顺序，按州或按国家对报纸进行排序。

text: Today's Front Pages does not archive or save front page images,
zh: [16] 《今日头版》不会存档或保存头版图片，

text: but it does archive newspapers from historically important dates, such as November 5, 2008.
zh: [17] 但它确实存档了具有历史意义日期的报纸，比如2008年11月5日。

text: That was one day after Barack Obama was elected president.
zh: [18] 那是巴拉克·奥巴马当选总统的次日。

text: Paul Sparrow says readers can learn a lot from the historical newspapers in the museum's collection.
zh: [19] 保罗·斯帕罗说，读者可以从博物馆馆藏的历史报纸中学到很多东西。

text: He says they will get a better understanding of the culture that existed long ago.
zh: [20] 他说，人们会因此更好地理解很久以前存在的那种文化。

text: The website shows only the front pages of general interest newspapers that publish daily.
zh: [21] 该网站只展示每日发行的大众类报纸的头版。

text: Student newspapers are not displayed.
zh: [22] 学生报纸不会被展示。

text: Some newspapers are not included because they do not have the technological ability
zh: [23] 有些报纸未被收录，是因为它们不具备

text: to send their front pages electronically
zh: [24] 以电子方式发送头版

text: to the museum, and others simply choose not to do so.
zh: [25] 到博物馆的技术条件，还有一些则是单纯选择不这么做。

text: The site displays the front pages of 836 newspapers from 93 countries.
zh: [26] 该网站展示了来自93个国家的836家报纸的头版。

text: It warns that the front pages are in their original unedited form
zh: [27] 网站提醒，这些头版都是未经编辑的原始版本，

text: and some may contain material that is deemed objectionable to some visitors.
zh: [28] 有些内容可能会被部分访问者认为不妥。

text: If you are in Washington, you can see many front pages
zh: [29] 如果你在华盛顿，你可以在博物馆内外看到许多

text: from around the world on display inside and just outside the museum.
zh: [30] 来自世界各地展出的头版。

text: Only about 10 % of the front pages the museum receives every day
zh: [31] 博物馆每天收到的头版中，只有大约10%

text: are displayed, but all are available online.
zh: [32] 会被展出，但所有头版都可以在网上查看。

text: You can read and hear our reports online at voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: [33] 你可以在voaspecialenglish.com上阅读和收听我们的报道。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Mario Ritter.
zh: [34] 我是马里奥·里特，为您带来美国之音慢速英语报道。
